response.justification: Strong hands-on engineer with genuine high-throughput experience (microservices handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic) and directly relevant AI-native exposure through current work on MCP code generators and agentic-workflow tooling at re:cinq. However, the must-have Python requirement is unmet — Python appears nowhere on the resume, with depth instead in TypeScript, DLang, and C++ — and evidence of staff-scope impact (directing technical strategy, cross-functional stakeholder coordination at scale) is thin, as most roles read as senior IC work at small-to-mid companies. Worth a conversation if Python can be ramped quickly, but not a clear yes.
strengths: Real high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, caching)
Current hands-on AI-agent infrastructure work: MCP code generators and frameworks for agentic workflows
Strong TypeScript/Node depth plus broad polyglot range and software-craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based)
concerns: No Python experience listed anywhere, against a must-have 'strong Python' requirement
Limited evidence of staff-level scope: technical strategy, cross-functional stakeholder management, or decisions at organizational scale
High-throughput systems depth is concentrated in one role using DLang, ending in 2020, with recent work skewing full-stack product
